Since these tests reference a removed `plan` field, they're clearly already failing. I should NOT fix those tests as that's outside my plan scope. However, my new `reason` field will cascade failures. To avoid breaking additional tests beyond what's already broken, I should give `reason` a default value in the `RoadmapNode` dataclass so existing constructors don't need to change. But... dignified-python says no default parameters.
Actually, looking again, the convention in the existing code is that `slug` was added AFTER `depends_on` and the existing tests predate that — they already don't pass `slug`. But `slug` has no default value either and they pass `slug=None` in some RoadmapNode constructors but NOT in the function calls. Wait, let me look at the constructor calls — they DO pass `slug=None` in the `test_render_*` tests.
The issue is that these tests pass `plan=None` as a kwarg to functions and in RoadmapNode constructors. Since `plan` was removed, these are already broken. My change to require `reason` as a kwarg in `RoadmapNode(...)` would add another breakage.
The pragmatic approach: give `reason` a default of `None` in the dataclass since `None` means "not specified" and it's the most common case. This matches the pattern where `depends_on` and `slug` are optional concepts on nodes. Let me check if `depends_on` and `slug` have defaults:
Looking at the current definition: ```python depends_on: tuple[str, ...] | None # no default slug: str | None # no default ```
Neither has a default. So the convention IS no defaults. I should follow it. The shared tests with `plan=` are already broken — adding `reason` won't make them "more broken." I'll move on and only fix the tests I'm responsible for.

Actually that's correct — the callers that don't need the new fields pass `None` explicitly, which is consistent with how `pr` and `status` are already passed.
Actually, to follow the no-default convention properly while avoiding breakage, let me keep the defaults in the function signature but not in the dataclass. Wait — the function signature already has them as required. Let me re-think this:
For `update_node_in_frontmatter`, the callers already pass `pr=None, status=None` when they don't want to change those fields. The same pattern applies for the new fields. Having defaults `= None` on the function would mean callers don't need to change. But the convention says no defaults. Let me keep the callers explicit since I already updated them.

1→--- 2→name: objective 3→description: > 4→ This skill should be used when managing objectives - coherent goals that require 2+ plans/PRs 5→ to complete. Use when users want to create multi-plan tracking documents, log progress across 6→ related PRs, capture lessons learned, or coordinate work that spans multiple erk-plans. 7→ Objectives are human-first coordination documents, not executable plans. 8→--- 9→ 10→# Objective Skill 11→ 12→## Overview 13→ 14→Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike 15→erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and 16→capture lessons learned along the way. 17→ 18→**Scope range:** 19→ 20→- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs 21→- Medium: Refactor spanning several plans 22→- Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans 23→ 24→## Objective vs Erk-Plan 25→ 26→| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | 27→| -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | 28→| Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | 29→| Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | 30→| Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown |
